issues.criterion: The Operator-Exception Rule's entire integrity guarantee (ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md Sec 5) is that a human approves the EXACT exceptions presented to them at the pending step -- not a set the generator later re-resolves independently. Entry 3 ("MECHANISM -- APPROVED GATE") is this contract's load-bearing proof of the approved-step invocation, yet it never compares the approved artifact's `exceptions[]` against the pending step's own `preflight.pending.json` `exceptions[]` from the same run.
issues.evidence: Built the exact two-step sequence in a disposable `cp -a` clone (never touching the real repo): ran the pending step exactly as entry 2 specifies (tolerate-list = v0.6.0-release-notes.md + .agent/contracts/043.json), which presented exceptions ['.agent/contracts/043.json', 'v0.6.0-release-notes.md'] in preflight.pending.json. Then, simulating a drifted/careless re-invocation, ran the approved step with an EXTRA untracked file (.agent/contracts/999-drift.json) added to the tolerate-list that the pending step never saw or presented -- a file the human operator therefore never reviewed. The approved-step invocation still returned exit 3 and wrote a preflight.json whose exceptions[] now include the drifted path (['.agent/contracts/043.json', '.agent/contracts/999-drift.json', '.agent/evidence/dogfood-eval-drift/', 'v0.6.0-release-notes.md']) plus a schema-valid, non-placeholder operator_approval block ('alice.chen', 'reviewed and approved the two pending exceptions'). Re-ran entry 3's LITERAL verification command (schema validation + operator_approval field checks only) against this drifted artifact: it printed PASS. Confirmed independently that none of the other 14 verification entries (including entries 9-12, the GATE-DEPENDENT post-implementation checks that inspect the real artifacts) compare preflight.pending.json's exceptions against the final preflight.json's exceptions either.
issues.required_fix: Add a verification entry (mechanism-proof AND a GATE-DEPENDENT post-implementation counterpart) asserting that the approved artifact's exceptions[] set equals the pending step's own preflight.pending.json exceptions[] set, modulo only the two well-understood, contract-documented self-reference additions (the pending step's own now-untracked output-directory path, and the approved step's own now-untracked evidence-directory path) -- and that ANY other divergent path fails the check loudly. Without this, a run can silently present one exception set to the human operator and record a different, larger one as 'approved'.

issues.criterion: Contract-internal consistency of what the deliverable set actually is: design_notes.two_step_gate_procedure and design_notes.no_evidence_duplication_of_verdict_artifacts make directly conflicting claims about whether .agent/evidence/<execution-id>/approved/{preflight.json,baseline.json} (the CLI's own emitted output at that path) persist on disk after the reconciliation hand-off.
issues.criterion_detail: design_notes.two_step_gate_procedure states the generator "COPIES (not moves, so the raw as-emitted evidence is preserved) exactly the two schema-gated files ... from .agent/evidence/<execution-id>/approved/ into the canonical .agent/reconciliation/<execution-id>/ location" -- i.e. cp, not mv, so the source files remain in the evidence directory. design_notes.no_evidence_duplication_of_verdict_artifacts states the opposite: "the evidence directory intentionally holds only the RAW, intermediate/as-emitted material ... -- not a second copy of the final preflight.json/baseline.json, which live once, canonically, under .agent/reconciliation/<execution-id>/". scope.files_create's five-item deliverable list also sides with the second claim (it lists only approved/collection-log.txt for the approved/ directory, omitting approved/preflight.json and approved/baseline.json entirely).
issues.evidence: Re-read contract lines 10 and 16 verbatim (design_notes.two_step_gate_procedure and design_notes.no_evidence_duplication_of_verdict_artifacts) side by side; the two statements cannot both be true of the same final working-tree state after implementation. Confirmed neither verification entry 11 ("evidence is stored by path") nor any other of the 15 entries checks for the PRESENCE or ABSENCE of .agent/evidence/<execution-id>/approved/{preflight.json,baseline.json} -- entry 11 only asserts pending/collection-log.txt, pending/preflight.pending.json, and approved/collection-log.txt are non-empty, so a generator following either reading of the contradiction would pass every one of the 15 entries unchanged.
issues.required_fix: Resolve the contradiction explicitly in the contract text: either (a) change the procedure to genuinely move (not copy) preflight.json/baseline.json out of the evidence approved/ directory into .agent/reconciliation/ -- matching files_create's omission and the no-duplication non-goal -- and correct the misleading 'not moves, so raw evidence is preserved' phrasing, or (b) add approved/preflight.json and approved/baseline.json to scope.files_create as deliberately duplicated raw evidence and revise the no-duplication design note to state the intentional exception and why. Either way, add a verification entry pinning whichever final state is chosen, so a generator cannot silently pick either interpretation undetected.

missing_acceptance_coverage: No verification entry (mechanism-proof or GATE-DEPENDENT) asserts pending/approved exception-set equality between preflight.pending.json and the final preflight.json -- see HIGH issue above (verification_index 3). This is the single most run-integrity-relevant gap for a feature whose entire justification is a real, human-gated operator-exception decision.

No verification entry pins whether .agent/evidence/<execution-id>/approved/{preflight.json,baseline.json} must exist or must not exist after implementation -- see MEDIUM issue above; the contract's own design_notes disagree with each other on this point and nothing tests it either way.
unsupported_claims: design_notes.two_step_gate_procedure's claim that copying (not moving) preflight.json/baseline.json 'preserves' the raw-as-emitted evidence in .agent/evidence/<execution-id>/approved/ is unsupported by, and directly contradicted by, design_notes.no_evidence_duplication_of_verdict_artifacts's claim that no such second copy exists; neither claim is backed by a verification entry (see MEDIUM issue above).
observations: ENTRY 6 (BOUNDED TOLERATE-LIST) CONFIRMED SOUND, TWO INDEPENDENT WAYS: reproduced the contract's own shell-side resolution logic against a disposable fixture carrying a genuine surprise untracked file (oops_unrelated.txt) -- correctly refused with 'correctly refused to tolerate: oops_unrelated.txt', PASS. Independently also ran the REAL CLI (not just the shell resolution logic) against the same fixture with the surprise file deliberately left OUT of --tolerate-untracked: the CLI itself independently refuses, returning exit 1 (FAIL) with blocking_findings ['untracked change not declared tolerated: oops_unrelated.txt']. Both the contract's own bounded-resolution logic and the underlying shipped CLI mechanism agree and correctly refuse to launder in an unauthorized path.

A3 ORDERING HAZARD (flat verify-evidence files vs. the real run) CONFIRMED REAL AND HARD, CONCUR WITH MODE A NON-BLOCKING CHARACTERIZATION: reproduced the real tree's current untracked state (v0.6.0-release-notes.md + .agent/contracts/043.json) in a disposable clone, added a single stray .agent/evidence/043-verify-01.txt (simulating a flat verification-evidence file captured before the real dogfood invocation runs), then ran the SAME pending-step invocation entry 2 specifies. Result: PREFLIGHT VERDICT: FAIL (1 blocking finding), exit 1, writing preflight.json (not preflight.pending.json) with blocking_findings=['untracked change not declared tolerated: .agent/evidence/043-verify-01.txt']. This proves that if any of this contract's own flat 043-verify-NN.txt evidence files exist in the real working tree at the moment either real dogfood invocation runs, the real run breaks outright (spurious FAIL instead of the expected PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS), because the bounded tolerate-list has no entry for the flat evidence-file convention. Concur with Mode A (A3) that this is non-blocking as a contract-approval matter since it is an orchestration/sequencing discipline issue, not a defect in the verification logic itself, and it fails LOUDLY rather than silently -- but recommend the generator sequence work so that the two real dogfood invocations complete BEFORE any flat verification-evidence capture is written into the real .agent/evidence/ directory, or clean transient .agent/evidence/043-verify-*.txt files between the disposable dry-run captures (entries 1-8) and the real production run.

A2 ORDERING HAZARD (DEBT.md edit timing) CONFIRMED REAL, HARD, AND MORE SEVERE THAN A3: reproduced the real untracked state in a disposable clone, edited docs/planning/DEBT.md FIRST (appending a test NDEBT-017 row, simulating the generator doing the DEBT.md edit before running the real preflight invocations), then ran the pending-step invocation. Result: exit 1 (FAIL), blocking_findings=['uncommitted tracked change (M): docs/planning/DEBT.md']. Unlike an untracked-file problem, this is UNCONDITIONAL: collect_git_clean_state_findings in tools/ecosystem_preflight.py treats any modified TRACKED file as blocking regardless of --tolerate-untracked content (tolerate-untracked only ever applies to '??' untracked lines, never to 'M' modified-tracked lines), so no tolerate-list flag can rescue an out-of-order DEBT.md edit. Concur with Mode A (A2) that this is non-blocking as a contract-approval matter (again, fails loudly, and the design_notes.two_step_gate_procedure already implies DEBT.md is a downstream bookkeeping step after the gated run completes) -- but given the guaranteed (not merely probable) failure mode and the real, human-consumed H-DOGFOOD-EXCEPTION gate at stake, recommend the contract's design_notes or non_goals be amended with one explicit sentence pinning this ordering as a MUST, rather than relying solely on out-of-band advisory knowledge that is not durably recorded anywhere in the contract text itself.

SENTINEL BLOCKLIST (entry 9) SELF-CAUGHT-BUG REGRESSION CONFIRMED HOLDING: re-ran the full two-step sequence with --operator-approver/--operator-authorization set to each of 'test-operator', 'jane', and 'test' in turn (all three are in entry 9's SENTINELS set, and 'jane' is notable because it is the exact fixture value entries 3 and 5 use in their own disposable dry-run proofs) -- entry 9's literal check correctly raised AssertionError('placeholder-looking approver', ...) for all three, confirming the sentinel list still catches its own precedent-fixture values as well as the generic 'test-operator'/'test' sentinels.

SENTINEL BLOCKLIST FABRICATION VECTOR (entry 9, Mode A's A1) CONFIRMED REAL, NO STRONGER CONTRACT-LEVEL BINDING FOUND: ran the same two-step sequence with a plausible, non-sentinel operator-approver/authorization string ('alice.chen' / 'reviewed and approved') that was never actually relayed by a real human operator in this test -- entry 9's check passed cleanly (no AssertionError), confirming a string-content sentinel blocklist can never distinguish a fabricated-but-plausible-looking approval from a genuine one. Considered whether a stronger contract-level binding is achievable without violating this contract's own non_goals (which forbid editing tools/ecosystem_preflight.py, any schema, or any ecosystem/*.md protocol document): the only structurally stronger binding would be cross-checking preflight.json's operator_approval fields against an INDEPENDENTLY-AUTHORED record the generator cannot itself write (e.g. an orchestrator-owned relay entry in run_state.json history) -- but no such durable-record convention currently exists in this framework for gate decisions, and inventing one is itself an architectural/protocol change outside this contract's non_goals and outside a single ~130-line feature's reasonable scope. Concur with Mode A: the contract's own non-fabrication non_goals text plus the orchestrator-reconciliation duty at Mode B is the best achievable binding today; do not re-block on this.

ENTRY 12 (DEBT ROWS, additive-only diff semantics) CONFIRMED SOUND UNDER THREE SCENARIOS: (a) a genuine correct usage -- appending exactly two new, properly-referenced NDEBT-017/018 rows at the end of the table -- passes the additive-only check, the presence check, and the evidence-path-reference check cleanly; (b) a MODIFIED pre-existing row (renaming 'NDEBT-016' to 'NDEBT-016-EDITED' in place) is correctly caught by the `git diff -U0` removed-line check; (c) inserting the two new rows in the MIDDLE of the table (not appended at the end) does NOT false-positive the additive-only check, since a pure line insertion produces no '-' lines under git's diff algorithm regardless of position.

ENTRY 8 (EXECUTION-ID COLLISION) CONFIRMED TO ACTUALLY FIRE, NOT JUST TRIVIALLY PASS: the contract's own command only demonstrates the no-collision case against the current real tree. Independently created a directory at the exact computed .agent/reconciliation/<execution-id> path in a disposable clone and confirmed the `[ -e ... ]` collision check correctly detects it.

ENTRY 4 (CLEAN PASS) AND ENTRY 7 (FLAG-SURFACE ANCHOR) reproduced literally and both behave exactly as claimed: a fully clean fixture yields exit 0 with a bare PASS carrying neither exceptions nor operator_approval; --help documents all eight flags the procedure depends on.

NDEBT-014 FIVE-CLASS SWEEP: (1) HEAD-anchored anti-vacuity -- entry 1 correctly uses `git ls-tree -r HEAD` / `git show HEAD:...`, avoiding the working-tree-anchored mutual-exclusivity defect class. (2) Two-part .agent immutability invariant -- entry 15 correctly uses the harmonized tracked-history-clean-plus-untracked-confined-to-glob-range form (034-043 window), not a fragile per-feature-ID exclude list. (3) vlib_section_grep line-wrap fragility -- not applicable; this contract's verification array uses direct shell/python assertions, not markdown section-scoped grepping. (4) Bare substring false-pass -- audited entry 9's SENTINELS check (exact set membership, not substring) and entry 6/entry-15's case-glob path matching (glob, not substring); no containing-word risk identified given the current, controlled NDEBT/feature-ID numbering scheme. (5) Natural-language paraphrase bound -- not applicable; no open-ended natural-language content assertion exists in this contract.

ESTIMATED LINE COUNT SPOT-CHECK: measured actual emitted artifact sizes in a disposable full two-step run -- preflight.json 27 lines (contract estimate 14-26, off by 1, immaterial), baseline.json 47 lines (matches contract estimate exactly), preflight.pending.json 18 lines (contract estimate 10-15, off by a few lines, immaterial), each collection-log.txt 5 lines (matches). No material discrepancy from the ~130-line total estimate.

MODE A DURABLE-RECORD GAP (recurring, not new): as with this evaluator's own prior 041-contract-review, no persisted Mode A verdict artifact exists on disk for feature 043 (.agent/validator/ still holds only 026-030's mode-a/mode-b pairs) -- this review necessarily proceeds from the orchestrator's chat-relayed characterization of Mode A's three advisories (A1/A2/A3) rather than a durable, independently re-readable Mode A artifact per AH-1. This is an orchestrator/validator process gap outside contract 043's own scope, not a defect of this contract, and was already flagged (unresolved) at feature 041's review -- noted again for continuity, non-blocking for this contract.

PROBE ISOLATION CONFIRMED: `git status --porcelain=v1` was checked on the real repository at /home/cnross/workspace02/nizam-framework before and after this entire review; it shows only the two pre-existing untracked entries (.agent/contracts/043.json, v0.6.0-release-notes.md) throughout, unchanged, aside from this review's own artifact at .agent/qa/043-contract-review.json. All disposable clones and fixtures were built and exercised exclusively under mktemp -d in the evaluator's scratchpad; no real repository path under ecosystem/, schema/, tools/, docs/, NIZAM.json, or any other .agent/ location was created, edited, or deleted, and no operator approval was ever presented as real -- every --operator-approver/--operator-authorization value used in this review was a clearly-labeled test string ('jane', 'test-operator', 'test', 'alice.chen') inside disposable fixtures only.

final_verdict.summary: NOT approved at revision 0. Adversarial two-step-gate testing against disposable clones confirms most of the 15-entry suite is sound: the pending/approved CLI mechanics, the bounded tolerate-list resolution (both its own shell logic and the underlying CLI's independent refusal), the sentinel blocklist's regression coverage of its own precedent fixture values, the additive-only DEBT.md diff check (including a mid-table-insertion edge case), the execution-id collision guard, the clean-pass mechanism, the flag-surface anchor, and the NDEBT-014 five-class sweep all hold up under direct empirical re-verification. Mode A's three non-blocking advisories (A1 sentinel-fabrication limitation, A2 DEBT.md-after-both-runs ordering, A3 flat-evidence-file ordering) were independently reproduced and confirmed real; this evaluator concurs with Mode A's non-blocking characterization for all three (A1 has no stronger achievable contract-level binding without out-of-scope protocol/schema changes; A2 and A3 both fail loudly rather than silently, though A2 in particular is a guaranteed, unconditional failure mode worth an explicit contract-text sentence as a low-cost improvement). However, this review surfaces two issues Mode A's advisories did not cover, both squarely within this evaluation's RUN INTEGRITY mandate: (1) HIGH -- no verification entry anywhere in the 15-entry suite asserts that the approved step's recorded exceptions[] equal the pending step's presented exceptions[] (modulo the two well-understood self-reference additions); empirically constructed a drifted approved-step invocation (an extra untracked file the pending step never presented to the operator) that still passes entry 3's literal check and validates cleanly end-to-end, meaning a run genuinely could present one exception set to the human operator and record a different, larger one as 'approved' undetected. This directly undermines the Operator-Exception Rule's core integrity guarantee that the gate is supposed to provide. (2) MEDIUM -- the contract's own design_notes contain a direct internal contradiction over whether the CLI's raw approved-step output (.agent/evidence/<execution-id>/approved/preflight.json and baseline.json) is preserved as a duplicate (per two_step_gate_procedure's explicit 'COPIES not moves... preserved' instruction) or must not exist as a duplicate (per no_evidence_duplication_of_verdict_artifacts and scope.files_create's omission of those two paths from the deliverable list) -- nothing in the 15-entry suite pins which reading is correct, so a generator could implement either and pass every check. required_state: generator_rework -- revise the contract to add a pending/approved exception-set-equality verification entry (mechanism-proof plus a GATE-DEPENDENT post-implementation counterpart) and resolve the copy-vs-duplicate contradiction (either genuinely moving the raw approved artifacts into .agent/reconciliation/ to match files_create and the no-duplication non-goal, or explicitly adding them to files_create with a revised non-goal and a pinning verification entry), then resubmit for a rev1 review. Given the real human operator gate (H-DOGFOOD-EXCEPTION) this feature exercises for real, closing the exception-set-equality gap before implementation is the single highest-priority fix.
